title: campanula breynina
intro: Campanula breynina, also known as Khyang or Wild Hound's Tongue, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Campanula breynina include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Campanula breynina can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it accessible for traditional medicinal purposes.
